preamble: Monophthongization of the diphthong [uo] to the close-mid back rounded vowel [o] through vowel merger and glide loss. This shift occurs in various Romance and Sino-Tibetan languages, representing a simplification pathway where the diphthong reduces to its back vowel component.

examples.note: In Wu Chinese, the diphthong [uo] frequently monophthongizes to the close-mid back rounded vowel [o] in various phonological contexts.
In some Romance dialects, [uo] simplifies to [o] through loss of the glide component, particularly in casual speech.
